meaningEn: Pumped up; feeling on a high
meaningJa: テンションが上がっている状態
exampleJa: 今日のライブまじあげぽよ！
exampleEn: Today's concert — I'm totally hyped!
socialConsequenceEn: Say this in 2024 and you'll sound like a time-traveler from 2009. People will smile. Politely.

meaningJa: マジ切れ5秒前
exampleJa: また遅刻？もうMK5なんだけど。
exampleRomaji: mata chikoku? mou emu-kei-go nan da kedo.
exampleEn: Late again? I'm 5 seconds from losing it.
socialConsequenceEn: Drop this and any Japanese person over 25 will laugh — it's peak Heisei gal code.

meaningJa: 超ベリーバッド（とても最悪）
exampleJa: テスト返ってきた、チョベリバ。
exampleEn: Got my test back. Absolute worst.
socialConsequenceEn: Pure 90s. Use it and you're basically cosplaying a Shibuya kogal.

tag.sourceNote: 超 + very bad; 1996 流行語

meaningJa: パーティー好きで盛り上がる人
exampleJa: 週末はパリピと朝まで。
exampleRomaji: shuumatsu wa pari-pi to asa made.
exampleEn: Weekends I'm out with the party crowd till dawn.
socialConsequenceEn: Totally fine today — you'll just sound like you love a good party.

tag.sourceNote: from English 'party people'; still in use

meaningJa: 感情が揺さぶられる、なつかしくて切ない
exampleJa: この曲、夕方に聴くとエモいわ。
exampleRomaji: kono kyoku, yuugata ni kiku to emo-i wa.
exampleEn: This song hits different at dusk — so emo.
socialConsequenceEn: Safe and current. You'll blend right in with Gen Z.

meaningEn: An all-purpose hype exclamation (no literal meaning)
meaningJa: ノリや勢いを表す掛け声（意味は薄い）
exampleJa: テンション上がってきた、卍！
exampleEn: Getting hyped — manji!!
socialConsequenceEn: Gen-Z teen energy. From an adult it's adorably try-hard.

meaningJa: 親友（マブダチの略）
exampleJa: あいつはマブやで。
exampleEn: That guy's my bestie.
socialConsequenceEn: 90s gal code for 'bestie'. Cute, and very retro now.

meaningJa: 軽い挨拶（ギャル）
exampleJa: チョリース、また明日ね。
exampleEn: Later, see ya tomorrow!
socialConsequenceEn: Peak 2000s gal greeting. Say it and you're a full Shibuya throwback.

meaningJa: どちらかというとアリ
exampleJa: その店ありよりのあり！
exampleEn: That place? Definitely a yes.
socialConsequenceEn: Reiwa schoolgirl speak. Fun with teens, baffling to adults.

meaningJa: 黒く日焼けしたギャルのスタイル
exampleJa: 昔ガングロめっちゃ流行った。
exampleEn: Ganguro was huge back in the day.
socialConsequenceEn: An iconic late-90s gal look. Pure nostalgia now.

meaningJa: 計算して可愛く振る舞う
exampleJa: あの子あざといけど可愛い。
exampleEn: She's so calculated-cute — but cute.
socialConsequenceEn: Totally current. A playful (mildly shady) compliment.

meaningEn: (A photo) makes you look way cuter
meaningJa: 写真でより可愛く写る
exampleJa: このアプリ盛れる！
exampleEn: This app makes me look amazing!
socialConsequenceEn: Everyday SNS slang. Use it freely about photos.

meaningJa: 超ベリーグッド。最高という意味
exampleJa: このネイルチョベリグじゃん！
exampleEn: These nails are the absolute best!
socialConsequenceEn: Pure 90s gal. Say it now and everyone knows your CD collection had MAX in it.

tag.sourceNote: 90s kogyaru staple, paired antonym of choberiba

meaningJa: 気分が最高に上がっている様子
exampleJa: 今日のクラブあげあげだったね！
exampleRomaji: kyou no kurabu age-age datta ne!
exampleEn: The club tonight had us so hyped!
socialConsequenceEn: Reads as a gal who still has a flip-phone charm. Charming, dated.

meaningJa: テンションが上がること
exampleJa: 好きな曲かかってテンアゲ！
exampleEn: My song came on and I'm pumped!
socialConsequenceEn: Still survives among younger gals. Mostly safe, slightly loud.

meaningJa: 気分が下がっている状態
exampleJa: テスト返ってきてさげぽよ…
exampleEn: Got my test back and I'm so down...
socialConsequenceEn: The gloomy twin of age-poyo. Peak 2010 gal-blog energy.

tag.sourceNote: antonym coinage of age-poyo, gal blog era

meaningJa: かわいいを崩した言い方
exampleJa: その子犬きゃわたん！
exampleEn: That puppy is so adorable!
socialConsequenceEn: Aggressively cutesy. Use sparingly or you'll sound like a 2012 purikura.

meaningJa: 金髪に染めた髪
exampleJa: 夏だしパツキンにしよっかな
exampleRomaji: natsu dashi patsukin ni shiyokka na
exampleEn: It's summer, maybe I'll go bleach-blonde.
socialConsequenceEn: Just describes the look. Says 'gal' louder than any word.

meaningJa: ヤマンバ系の派手なギャルメイク
exampleJa: あの頃マンバ全盛だったよね
exampleRomaji: ano koro manba zensei datta yo ne
exampleEn: Back then the manba look was everywhere.
socialConsequenceEn: A whole subculture: dark tan, white lips, neon. Pure Shibuya 2004.

meaningJa: 高校生のギャル文化
exampleJa: ママ昔コギャルだったらしい
exampleEn: Apparently my mom was a kogyaru back then.
socialConsequenceEn: Loose socks, tanned, mini skirt. The original gal blueprint.

meaningEn: Flashy and flirty; shallow (esp. a guy)
meaningJa: 軽薄で遊んでそうな様子
exampleJa: あの先輩ちょっとチャラいよね
exampleRomaji: ano senpai chotto charai yo ne
exampleEn: That senior is kind of a player, huh.
socialConsequenceEn: Still totally current. Mild insult, half affectionate.

meaningEn: Insane; off the charts (good or bad)
meaningJa: 半端ない、の崩れた形
exampleJa: 今日の暑さパネェ
exampleEn: Today's heat is insane.
socialConsequenceEn: Rough gal-guy energy. Reads early-2010s but still understood.

meaningJa: プリントシール機で撮る写真
exampleJa: 帰りにプリクラ撮ろ！
exampleEn: Let's take purikura on the way home!
socialConsequenceEn: Totally alive. A gal-culture institution, now everyone's word.

meaningJa: つけまつげの略
exampleJa: つけま取れかけてるよ
exampleEn: Your false lashes are coming off.
socialConsequenceEn: Pure gal makeup vocab. Still used, very on-brand.

meaningJa: ラインストーン等で飾ること
exampleJa: ケースめっちゃデコったの
exampleEn: I totally bedazzled my phone case.
socialConsequenceEn: Deco-phone era flashback. Still works for nails.

meaningJa: かっこいい男性
exampleJa: 新しいバイト先イケメン多い
exampleEn: Lots of hot guys at my new job.
socialConsequenceEn: Fully mainstream now. Gal-origin, everyone's word.

meaningJa: 格好悪い、野暮ったい
exampleJa: その組み合わせちょいダサいかも
exampleEn: That combo is kind of lame, maybe.
socialConsequenceEn: Timeless. Safe to use anywhere, anytime.

meaningJa: 面白い、笑える
exampleJa: それマジでウケるんだけど
exampleRomaji: sore maji de ukeru n da kedo
exampleEn: That's seriously hilarious.
socialConsequenceEn: Still everywhere. Gal-flavored but universal now.

meaningEn: Meh; iffy; not great not terrible
meaningJa: 良くも悪くも言えない、微妙
exampleJa: あの店の味ビミョーだった
exampleRomaji: ano mise no aji bimyou datta
exampleEn: That place's food was kinda meh.
socialConsequenceEn: Totally current and useful. Zero risk.

meaningJa: 面倒くさいをおしゃれに言った形
exampleJa: 課題多すぎメンディー
exampleEn: Too much homework, what a pain.
socialConsequenceEn: Reads young and a bit try-hard, but still circulating.

meaningJa: 信じられない、あってはならない
exampleJa: ドタキャンとかありえないんだけど
exampleRomaji: dotakyan toka arienai n da kedo
exampleEn: Cancelling last minute? Unbelievable.
socialConsequenceEn: Gal-drama classic, fully mainstream. Safe.

meaningJa: 不良、ツッパリ
exampleJa: 昔ヤンキーだった先生
exampleEn: A teacher who used to be a delinquent.
socialConsequenceEn: Still understood by everyone. A cultural staple.

meaningEn: The vibe / mood; going along with it
meaningJa: その場の勢いや雰囲気
exampleJa: ノリで買っちゃった
exampleEn: I bought it just going with the vibe.
socialConsequenceEn: Everyday word now. Gal energy at its root.

meaningJa: かっこいい、イケている
exampleJa: その髪型イケてるじゃん
exampleEn: That hairstyle looks great on you.
socialConsequenceEn: Reads slightly 00s but everyone gets it.

meaningJa: ありがとうございますの崩れた形
exampleJa: おごってくれてあざーす！
exampleEn: Thanks for treating me!
socialConsequenceEn: Casual and current, slightly rough. Friends only.

meaningJa: 盛り上がった時のかけ声
exampleJa: 金曜だしうぇーい！
exampleEn: It's Friday, wooo!
socialConsequenceEn: The sound of party people (pari-pi). Joyfully obnoxious.

meaningJa: ギャル文化の男性版
exampleJa: 渋谷でギャル男よく見たな
exampleRomaji: shibuya de gyaru-o yoku mita na
exampleEn: Used to see lots of gyaru-o in Shibuya.
socialConsequenceEn: Tanned, spiky hair, deep V. The male Shibuya 00s look.

meaningJa: 髪やメイクを派手に盛ること
exampleJa: 今日は盛り盛りで行く
exampleEn: Going all-out glam today.
socialConsequenceEn: Core gal aesthetic word. Still used for glam looks.

tag.sourceNote: moru noun form, gal glam term

meaningJa: しっかり、たっぷりと
exampleJa: 今日はがっつり食べたい
exampleEn: I want to eat a ton today.
socialConsequenceEn: Fully mainstream now. Safe everywhere.

meaningJa: あいさつの崩れた形
exampleJa: ちょりっす、元気？
exampleEn: Yo, you good?
socialConsequenceEn: Sibling of choriisu. Peak gal-guy greeting energy.

meaningJa: ほとんど、おおよそ
exampleJa: 準備ほぼほぼ終わった
exampleEn: Prep is pretty much done.
socialConsequenceEn: Now mainstream/business-safe. Started as casual youth talk.

meaningJa: うっとうしい、面倒
exampleJa: 通知多すぎてうざい
exampleEn: Too many notifications, so annoying.
socialConsequenceEn: Totally standard now. Gal/young origin.

meaningJa: 気持ち悪い
exampleJa: その虫きもいんだけど
exampleRomaji: sono mushi kimoi n da kedo
exampleEn: That bug is so gross.
socialConsequenceEn: Universal now. Can sting — use on bugs, not people.

meaningJa: 完全に無視すること
exampleJa: 既読なのにガン無視された
exampleEn: They read it and totally ignored me.
socialConsequenceEn: Still everywhere, especially over text drama.

meaningEn: A local; someone from your hometown
meaningJa: 地元の友達・地元民
exampleJa: 盆はじもてぃーと集まる
exampleEn: I meet up with my hometown crew at Obon.
socialConsequenceEn: Friendly gal-flavored slang, still circulating.

meaningJa: 安っぽく派手な様子
exampleJa: ちょっとじゃんがらすぎかな
exampleEn: Maybe a bit too gaudy?
socialConsequenceEn: Niche gal slang — flag for a native check.

tag.sourceNote: less-attested gal term for gaudy; needs review
